name: Mirabel from Casas de Millán – circular by Castillo de Mirabel and Embalse de Arroyo Boquerón
name_original: Mirabel desde Casas de Millán – circular por Castillo de Mirabel y Embalse de Arroyo Boquerón
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Casas de Millán traverse the last mountain spurs of the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve, offering diverse landscapes. The region features traditional Iberian *dehesas* dotted with oak trees, extending towards the Tagus River and Monfragüe National Park. Cyclists can explore extensive areas of matorral and holm oak groves, with routes providing territorial views from the Sierra de Santa Maria. This area of Cáceres is characterized by its natural biodiversity and varied terrain. Best touring cycling routes around Casas De Millán The most popular touring cycling route is Mirabel from Casas de Millán – circular by Castillo de Mirabel and Embalse de Arroyo Boquerón, a 29.0 miles (46.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Castillo de Mirabel and the Embalse de Arroyo Boquerón. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Floripes Tower – Puente de Alconétar loop from Casas de Millán, a difficult 55.1 miles (88.7 km) path. This route features historical landmarks like Floripes Tower and the Puente de Alconétar, crossing varied landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Floripes Tower – Vía de la Plata loop from Cañaveral, a 68.6 miles (110.5 km) trail leading through historical areas and open dehesa, often completed in about 7 hours 41 minutes. Touring cycling around Casas De Millán is defined by its *dehesas*, oak groves, and mountain spurs within the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve. The network offers options primarily for experienced cyclists, with most routes rated as difficult. The routes in Casas De Millán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Casas De Millán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Casas De Millán?
A: There are 14 touring cycling routes available around Casas De Millán. These routes offer diverse experiences through the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Casas De Millán?
A: The majority of touring cycling routes in Casas De Millán are considered difficult, with 12 out of 14 routes falling into this category. There are also 2 moderate routes, but no easy routes, indicating the area is best suited for experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling in Casas De Millán?
A: Touring cycling routes in Casas De Millán traverse the last mountain spurs of the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve. You'll encounter traditional Iberian &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; dotted with oak trees, extensive areas of matorral, and holm oak groves. Routes often provide impressive territorial views from the Sierra de Santa Maria and extend towards the Tagus River and Monfragüe National Park.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by significant historical landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Floripes Tower – Puente de Alconétar loop from Casas de Millán&lt;/a&gt; features the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5676800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Floripes Tower&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3796608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Benito Gothic Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The region also has Roman vestiges and the notable Chapel of Our Lady of Tebas.

Q: Can I see any natural features or viewpoints on the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The routes offer views of natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/656148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara-Garrovillas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5406515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talaván Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5876460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta del Fraile&lt;/a&gt; waterfall and enjoy panoramic views from spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5878617" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Lobo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the touring routes in Casas De Millán?
A: The touring cycling routes in Casas De Millán are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging yet rewarding nature of the rides through dehesas and oak groves.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Casas De Millán?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110762728/mirabel-from-casas-de-millan-circular-by-castillo-de-mirabel-and-embalse-de-arroyo-boqueron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirabel from Casas de Millán – circular by Castillo de Mirabel and Embalse de Arroyo Boquerón&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 29.0-mile (46.6 km) difficult trail offering views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5676800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Mirabel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Casas De Millán?
A: Given that most routes in Casas De Millán are rated as difficult, the area is primarily suited for experienced cyclists. There are no routes specifically categorized as easy, and only two are moderate. Families with younger children or those seeking very gentle rides might find the terrain challenging.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Casas De Millán?
A: Casas de Millán, located in Extremadura, experiences hot summers. Spring and autumn generally offer more pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, allowing for comfortable exploration of the dehesas and natural reserves. Winter can also be suitable, though some days might be cooler.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling trails in Casas De Millán?
A: While specific parking areas for each trail are not detailed, Casas de Millán is a small village. Parking is generally available within the village itself, allowing access to routes that start or pass through the area. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are there options for long-distance touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, the region offers several long-distance options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Floripes Tower – Vía de la Plata loop from Cañaveral&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 68.6-mile (110.5 km) trail, leading through historical areas and open dehesa, often taking over 7 hours to complete.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes using public transport to Casas De Millán?
A: Information on specific public transport options for cyclists to Casas De Millán is limited. While bus services might connect to larger towns in the Cáceres region, it's recommended to verify if bikes are permitted on board. For broader regional cycling information, you might find resources on sites like &lt;a href="https://visitambroz.es/en/rutas-bici-caceres/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitambroz.es&lt;/a&gt;, which covers cycling routes in the Cáceres area.
tips:
The path goes into the pasture, taking you away from the noise of the highway. It is a very picturesque and fun area due to the small slides created by the undulation of the earth itself.

If you feel like resting, the shade of a holm oak can be your ideal place. The views you get of the area are worth it.
It is a swamp with a large dam formed in the Torrecilla del Carmen meadow. The contrast of the bluish color of the reservoir and the green of the pasture form a very beautiful image to remember.

The ruins of Castillo de Mirabel are an imposing defensive fortress built on the western hills of the Monfragüe National Park. If you choose to go up to the castle, you will be treated to wonderful views of the Tagus Valley landscape below.
